misconceptions.name: AbstractClassMustImplementAbstractMethod
misconceptions.shortDescription: An abstract class must implement all abstract methods defined in its superclass

misconceptions.name: AbstractClassNoImplementation
misconceptions.shortDescription: An abstract class cannot contain implemented methods

misconceptions.name: AddMemberAtRuntime
misconceptions.shortDescription: Set of class members can change at runtime

misconceptions.name: AllClassesHaveDefaultConstructor
misconceptions.shortDescription: All classes automatically get a no-argument constructor

misconceptions.name: AnyClassException
misconceptions.shortDescription: Any class can be an exception class

misconceptions.name: ArithmeticPlusPrecedes
misconceptions.shortDescription: Addition has higher precedence than string concatenation

misconceptions.name: ArrayAccessWithParentheses
misconceptions.shortDescription: Parenthesis are used to access an element in an array

misconceptions.name: ArrayAllocationWithoutNew
misconceptions.shortDescription: Arrays are created without the new keyword

misconceptions.name: ArrayBracketCountIsLength
misconceptions.shortDescription: The number of brackets in an array type or an array initializer corresponds to the length of the array

misconceptions.name: ArrayElementTypeRepeats
misconceptions.shortDescription: The type of a multi-dimensional array is written as T[] T[] T[]

misconceptions.name: ArrayElementsUntyped
misconceptions.shortDescription: Elements of arrays are untyped

misconceptions.name: ArrayHasLengthMethod
misconceptions.shortDescription: To get the length of an array, one needs to call its length method

misconceptions.name: ArrayInitializerContentsInBrackets
misconceptions.shortDescription: Array initializers list the elements in square brackets

misconceptions.name: ArrayLengthCannotBeZero
misconceptions.shortDescription: An array cannot have a length of 0 elements

misconceptions.name: ArrayLengthPartOfType
misconceptions.shortDescription: The length of an array is part of its type

misconceptions.name: ArrayListIsArray
misconceptions.shortDescription: ArrayLists are arrays

misconceptions.name: ArrayRankIsLength
misconceptions.shortDescription: Array rank and array length are the same thing

misconceptions.name: ArrayRankNotPartOfType
misconceptions.shortDescription: The rank of an array is not part of its type

misconceptions.name: ArraysGrow
misconceptions.shortDescription: Arrays can grow dynamically

misconceptions.name: AssignCompares
misconceptions.shortDescription: = compares two values

misconceptions.name: AssignmentCopiesObject
misconceptions.shortDescription: Assignment copies the object

misconceptions.name: AssignmentNotExpression
misconceptions.shortDescription: An assignment a=b is not an expression

misconceptions.name: BaseCaseSelfRecursive
misconceptions.shortDescription: The base case of a structural recursion consists of a recursive self-call

misconceptions.name: BoxedNull
misconceptions.shortDescription: Passing null to a wrapper class constructor creates an object representing the absence of a value

misconceptions.name: CallNotStaticallyChecked
misconceptions.shortDescription: One can invoke a method on a reference of a class that does not have that method

misconceptions.name: CallOnPrimitive
misconceptions.shortDescription: One can invoke a method on primitive values

misconceptions.name: CallRequiresVariable
misconceptions.shortDescription: One needs a variable to invoke a method

misconceptions.name: CallWithoutFrame
misconceptions.shortDescription: A method invocation does not necessarily allocate a stack frame

misconceptions.name: CallerFrameContainsCalleeFormal
misconceptions.shortDescription: Stack frame of caller includes variables for callee's formal parameters

misconceptions.name: CannotChainMemberAccesses
misconceptions.shortDescription: Member accesses cannot be chained together

misconceptions.name: CannotChainMemberToConstructor
misconceptions.shortDescription: Method calls or field accesses cannot be chained to a constructor invocation

misconceptions.name: CatchAlwaysExecutes
misconceptions.shortDescription: Catch blocks always get executed

misconceptions.name: CatchProvidesOptions
misconceptions.shortDescription: Only the necessary part of a catch block executes

misconceptions.name: ChainedMethodsNotCalledFromOutside
misconceptions.shortDescription: Chained methods are all called on the object at the beginning of the chain

misconceptions.name: CharNotNumeric
misconceptions.shortDescription: Char is not a numeric type

misconceptions.name: ComparisonWithBooleanLiteral
misconceptions.shortDescription: To test whether an expression is true or false, one must compare it to true or to false

misconceptions.name: CompositeExpressionsUntyped
misconceptions.shortDescription: Expressions that consist of multiple parts have no type

misconceptions.name: ConcreteClassMustOverride
misconceptions.shortDescription: A concrete class needs to implement all methods declared in its abstract superclasses

misconceptions.name: ConcreteClassOnlyImplementClassAbstract
misconceptions.shortDescription: A concrete class only needs to implement those abstract methods it inherits from abstract superclasses

misconceptions.name: ConcreteClassOnlyImplementDirectAbstract
misconceptions.shortDescription: A concrete class only needs to implement abstract methods declared in its direct supertypes

misconceptions.name: ConcreteClassOnlyImplementInterfaceAbstract
misconceptions.shortDescription: A concrete class only needs to implement those abstract methods it inherits from interfaces

misconceptions.name: ConditionOneOutEdge
misconceptions.shortDescription: A condition node in a control-flow graph can have a single outgoing edge

misconceptions.name: ConditionalIsSequence
misconceptions.shortDescription: If-else is equivalent to sequence of two ifs

misconceptions.name: ConstructorAllocates
misconceptions.shortDescription: The constructor allocates the object

misconceptions.name: ConstructorParameterIsField
misconceptions.shortDescription: Formal constructor parameters are equivalent to instance variables

misconceptions.name: ConstructorReturnsObject
misconceptions.shortDescription: Constructors need to return objects

misconceptions.name: ConstructorWithoutNew
misconceptions.shortDescription: One can write the constructor name, without new, to instantiate a class

misconceptions.name: ControlledLocalAccess
misconceptions.shortDescription: One can control access to local variables using access modifiers

misconceptions.name: DeferredReturn
misconceptions.shortDescription: A return statement in the middle of a method doesn't return immediately

misconceptions.name: ElsIf
misconceptions.shortDescription: There is an elsif keyword for multi-way conditional statements

misconceptions.name: ElseAlwaysExecutes
misconceptions.shortDescription: The else branch of an if-else statement always executes

misconceptions.name: EqualityOperatorComparesObjectsValues
misconceptions.shortDescription: o==p compares the objects referred to by variables o and p

misconceptions.name: EqualsComparesReferences
misconceptions.shortDescription: o.equals(p) compares the references stored in the variables o and p

misconceptions.name: EvaluationResultsArePrinted
misconceptions.shortDescription: Evaluating an expression means outputting its result

misconceptions.name: ExceptionRoot
misconceptions.shortDescription: Exception is the top-most exception class

misconceptions.name: ExpressionAssigns
misconceptions.shortDescription: An expression that reads a variable also updates its value after the evaluation

misconceptions.name: ExpressionsDynamicallyTyped
misconceptions.shortDescription: One has to evaluate an expression to determine its type

misconceptions.name: FinalReferenceImpliesImmutability
misconceptions.shortDescription: An object referred to by a final variable is an immutable object

misconceptions.name: ForEachIteratesOverIndices
misconceptions.shortDescription: An enhanced for loop iterates over the indices of an array or a collection

misconceptions.name: ForEachTraversesRecursiveStructure
misconceptions.shortDescription: For-each loops know how to traverse any recursive data structure

misconceptions.name: ForEachVariableIsElement
misconceptions.shortDescription: One can assign to the variable of an enhanced for statement to store a value in the corresponding array or collection element

misconceptions.name: ForIsConditional
misconceptions.shortDescription: The body of a for statement executes at most once

misconceptions.name: ForVariableScopeBeyondLoop
misconceptions.shortDescription: The scope of variables declared in a for loop header extends beyond the loop

misconceptions.name: FrameIsClassInstance
misconceptions.shortDescription: A stack frame is the same as an instance of a class

misconceptions.name: BaseCaseNotNeeded
misconceptions.shortDescription: Recursive computations do not necessarily need a base case

misconceptions.name: IfIsLoop
misconceptions.shortDescription: The body of an if statement executes repeatedly, as long as the condition holds

misconceptions.name: IfRequiresElse
misconceptions.shortDescription: Every if statement requires an else

misconceptions.name: ImmutableRequiresFinalParameters
misconceptions.shortDescription: Immutable classes need final constructor/method parameters

misconceptions.name: ImplicitInterfaceImplementation
misconceptions.shortDescription: Java implicitly produces implementations of any methods a class inherits from the interfaces it implements

misconceptions.name: InlineCallInExpressionTree
misconceptions.shortDescription: The expression tree of an expression involving a call inlines the call's computation of the returned value

misconceptions.name: InlineVariableInExpressionTree
misconceptions.shortDescription: The expression tree of an expression involving a variable inlines the variable's definition

misconceptions.name: IntegerDivisionToRational
misconceptions.shortDescription: Dividing two integers can produce a rational number

misconceptions.name: InterfaceExtendClass
misconceptions.shortDescription: An interface can extend a class

misconceptions.name: InterproceduralEdge
misconceptions.shortDescription: In an intra-procedural control-flow graph, method call nodes point to the called method

misconceptions.name: LargeIntegerLong
misconceptions.shortDescription: Large integer numbers have type long

misconceptions.name: LiteralNoExpression
misconceptions.shortDescription: A literal is not an expression

misconceptions.name: LiteralString
misconceptions.shortDescription: When passing a literal string as argument to a method, no quotes are needed

misconceptions.name: LocalVariablesAutoInitialized
misconceptions.shortDescription: Local variables are automatically initialized

misconceptions.name: LoopBodyScopeImpliesLoopLifetime
misconceptions.shortDescription: Lifetime of variables declared in a loop body extends across all loop iterations

misconceptions.name: LoopTerminatingCondition
misconceptions.shortDescription: For and while loops end when the condition becomes true

misconceptions.name: MapIsMultiMap
misconceptions.shortDescription: Maps can store multiple values for a given key

misconceptions.name: MapPutNoOverwrite
misconceptions.shortDescription: Map.put with an existing key does nothing

misconceptions.name: MapToBooleanWithConditionalOperator
misconceptions.shortDescription: To map a boolean expression to a boolean, a conditional operator is necessary

misconceptions.name: MapToBooleanWithIf
misconceptions.shortDescription: To map a boolean expression to a boolean, an if statement is necessary

misconceptions.name: MethodAsField
misconceptions.shortDescription: Each object contains its own special fields for all of its methods

misconceptions.name: MethodWithoutReturnType
misconceptions.shortDescription: A method declaration does not need to include a return type

misconceptions.name: MethodsWithoutClass
misconceptions.shortDescription: Methods can be defined outside a class

misconceptions.name: MultiReferenceVariable
misconceptions.shortDescription: A reference variable can point to multiple objects

misconceptions.name: MultiValueVariable
misconceptions.shortDescription: A variable can contain more than one value

misconceptions.name: MultidimensionalArray
misconceptions.shortDescription: A multi-dimensional array is one thing

misconceptions.name: MultipleSuperclasses
misconceptions.shortDescription: A class can have multiple superclasses

misconceptions.name: MustInitializeFieldInConstructor
misconceptions.shortDescription: Constructors must assign values to all fields

misconceptions.name: NamedTypeParameter
misconceptions.shortDescription: To instantiate a generic type, one needs to specify type parameter names as well as types

misconceptions.name: NestedObjectsImplyNestedClasses
misconceptions.shortDescription: If objects are part of a containment hierarchy, their classes are nested, too

misconceptions.name: NestedPackages
misconceptions.shortDescription: Packages can contain other packages

misconceptions.name: NoAtomicExpression
misconceptions.shortDescription: Expressions must consist of more than one piece

misconceptions.name: NoCallOnStringLiteral
misconceptions.shortDescription: One cannot invoke methods on String literals

misconceptions.name: NoCastIfSameSize
misconceptions.shortDescription: If a variable is at least as big (bit-width) as a value, then no cast is needed to a assign the value to the variable

misconceptions.name: NoCharEscape
misconceptions.shortDescription: \ is a normal character in char and String literals

misconceptions.name: NoEmptyConstructor
misconceptions.shortDescription: A constructor must do something

misconceptions.name: NoFieldInheritance
misconceptions.shortDescription: An object contains only the fields declared in its class

misconceptions.name: NoFieldlessObjects
misconceptions.shortDescription: Objects without instance variables can't exist

misconceptions.name: NoFloatLiterals
misconceptions.shortDescription: There are no float literals

misconceptions.name: NoImplicitWidening
misconceptions.shortDescription: Smaller types are never automatically converted into bigger ones without an explicit cast

misconceptions.name: NoInsideMethodCallInConstructor
misconceptions.shortDescription: It is foirbidden to call other methods on the same object while inside its constructor

misconceptions.name: NoJaggedArrays
misconceptions.shortDescription: Multi-dimensional arrays have a rectangular shape

misconceptions.name: NoLocalVariables
misconceptions.shortDescription: There are no local variables

misconceptions.name: NoLongLiterals
misconceptions.shortDescription: There are no long literals

misconceptions.name: NoMethodInheritance
misconceptions.shortDescription: Subclasses inherit fields but not methods

misconceptions.name: NoReservedWords
misconceptions.shortDescription: Every sequence of letters and digits starting with a letter can be used as an identifier

misconceptions.name: NoShortCircuit
misconceptions.shortDescription: && and || always evaluate both operands

misconceptions.name: NoSingleLogicAnd
misconceptions.shortDescription: & is only a bitwise AND

misconceptions.name: NoStringToString
misconceptions.shortDescription: One cannot invoke toString() on a String

misconceptions.name: NullIsObject
misconceptions.shortDescription: null is an object

misconceptions.name: NullPointerExceptionCompileTime
misconceptions.shortDescription: NullPointerExceptions are detected at compile time

misconceptions.name: NumericToBooleanCoercion
misconceptions.shortDescription: Numeric types can be coerced to boolean

misconceptions.name: ObjectLabeledWithConstructorSignature
misconceptions.shortDescription: In stack and heap diagrams, an object on the heap is labeled like a stack frame of a constructor call

misconceptions.name: ObjectLabeledWithVariableName
misconceptions.shortDescription: In stack and heap diagrams, an object on the heap is labeled with the name of the variable that refers to it

misconceptions.name: ObjectsMustBeNamed
misconceptions.shortDescription: A variable is needed to instantiate an object

misconceptions.name: OnlyInnermostArrayElements
misconceptions.shortDescription: Only the elements of the innermost array of a multi-dimensional array are accessible

misconceptions.name: OnlyRuntimeLibraryPackages
misconceptions.shortDescription: Only the creators of the JDK can create packages

misconceptions.name: OutOfBoundsElementsAreNull
misconceptions.shortDescription: Out-of-bounds array elements are null

misconceptions.name: OutsideInMethodNesting
misconceptions.shortDescription: Nested method calls are invoked outside in

misconceptions.name: ParenthesesOnlyIfArgument
misconceptions.shortDescription: () are optional for method calls without arguments

misconceptions.name: PreIncrementBeforeLoop
misconceptions.shortDescription: Pre-increment in update part of for loop means increment before loop body

misconceptions.name: PrimitiveIsObject
misconceptions.shortDescription: Primitive values are heap objects

misconceptions.name: PrimitiveTypeParameter
misconceptions.shortDescription: Type parameters of generic types can be assigned primitive types

misconceptions.name: PrimitiveVariablesDynamicallyTyped
misconceptions.shortDescription: The type of a primitive variable depends on its value

misconceptions.name: PrintNewLineFirst
misconceptions.shortDescription: println(...) starts a new line and then prints

misconceptions.name: PrivateAccessibleInSubclass
misconceptions.shortDescription: Private members of a superclass are accessible from a subclass

misconceptions.name: PrivateFieldsImplyImmutability
misconceptions.shortDescription: A class where all fields are private is immutable

misconceptions.name: PrivateFromOtherInstance
misconceptions.shortDescription: An object cannot access private members of other objects of the same class

misconceptions.name: PrivateFromStatic
misconceptions.shortDescription: Static methods cannot access private members of instances of same class

misconceptions.name: PrivateMeansFinal
misconceptions.shortDescription: A private field cannot be changed

misconceptions.name: RationalLiterals
misconceptions.shortDescription: Rational fractions are literals

misconceptions.name: RecursiveActivationsShareFrame
misconceptions.shortDescription: Recursive calls of a method share a stack frame

misconceptions.name: RecursiveCallSiteNoReturn
misconceptions.shortDescription: Tail-recursive call sites have no continuation

misconceptions.name: RecursiveMethodImpliesRecursiveType
misconceptions.shortDescription: A class with a recursive method represents part of a recursive data structure

misconceptions.name: RecursiveMethodNeedsIfElse
misconceptions.shortDescription: A recursive method needs to contain an if-else statement

misconceptions.name: ReferenceIntoStack
misconceptions.shortDescription: References can point into the stack

misconceptions.name: ReferenceLabeledWithDynamicType
misconceptions.shortDescription: In stack and heap diagrams, reference variables are labeled with the types of the objects they refer to

misconceptions.name: ReferenceToBooleanCoercion
misconceptions.shortDescription: Every reference type can be coerced to boolean

misconceptions.name: ReferenceToIntegerConversion
misconceptions.shortDescription: One can cast between references and ints

misconceptions.name: ReferenceToVariable
misconceptions.shortDescription: References can point to variables

misconceptions.name: ReferringToRecursiveStructureMakesRecursive
misconceptions.shortDescription: A class referring to a recursive data structure is (indirectly) part of that recursion as well

misconceptions.name: ReturnCall
misconceptions.shortDescription: Return statements need () around the return value

misconceptions.name: ReturnNoOutEdge
misconceptions.shortDescription: In a control-flow graph, a return statement has no outgoing edge

misconceptions.name: ReturnUnwindsMultipleFrames
misconceptions.shortDescription: A return statement can unwind multiple call stack frames

misconceptions.name: RightToLeftChaining
misconceptions.shortDescription: Chained accesses are invoked from right to left

misconceptions.name: RuntimeExceptionChecked
misconceptions.shortDescription: RuntimeExceptions are checked exceptions

misconceptions.name: SemanticEquivalenceImpliesSyntacticEquivalence
misconceptions.shortDescription: Two pieces of code with the same observable behavior have to be implemented in the same way

misconceptions.name: SetterIsStatic
misconceptions.shortDescription: Setter methods are static

misconceptions.name: SingleQuoteString
misconceptions.shortDescription: String literals can be in single quotes

misconceptions.name: StackTraceIsCallHistory
misconceptions.shortDescription: A stack trace is the sequence of previously called methods

misconceptions.name: StatementMultipleOutEdges
misconceptions.shortDescription: A non-condition node in a control-flow graph can have multiple outgoing edges

misconceptions.name: StaticCallPolymorphic
misconceptions.shortDescription: A static method call is dispatched polymorphically at runtime based on the argument types

misconceptions.name: StaticDispatch
misconceptions.shortDescription: The method to be called is determined by the static type

misconceptions.name: StaticFrameInGlobals
misconceptions.shortDescription: Stack frame of static method calls lives in the global memory area

misconceptions.name: StringLengthField
misconceptions.shortDescription: One can know the length of a String object by accessing its length field

misconceptions.name: StringLiteralNoObject
misconceptions.shortDescription: One needs to call the String constructor to get a String object from a literal

misconceptions.name: StringPlusStringifiesExpression
misconceptions.shortDescription: String concatenation stringifies non-String operand expressions

misconceptions.name: StringRepetitionOperator
misconceptions.shortDescription: The multiplication operator can repeat a String a number of times

misconceptions.name: SubtypeCompatibleWithSupertype
misconceptions.shortDescription: A variable of a subtype can reference an object of a supertype

misconceptions.name: SuperAlwaysHasParentheses
misconceptions.shortDescription: To call a method on a superclass, parentheses are needed after the keyword super

misconceptions.name: SuperNotFirstStatement
misconceptions.shortDescription: super() can be called anywhere in the constructor of a subclass

misconceptions.name: SuperclassObjectAllocated
misconceptions.shortDescription: When instantiating an object of a subclass, an object of a superclass is also allocated

misconceptions.name: SupertypeIncompatibleWithSubtype
misconceptions.shortDescription: A variable of a supertype cannot reference an object of a subtype

misconceptions.name: TargetTyping
misconceptions.shortDescription: The type of a numerical expression depends on the type expected by the surrounding context

misconceptions.name: ThisAsField
misconceptions.shortDescription: this is a special field in the object

misconceptions.name: ThisAssignable
misconceptions.shortDescription: One can assign to this

misconceptions.name: ThisCanBeNull
misconceptions.shortDescription: this can be null

misconceptions.name: ThisChildInCallExpression
misconceptions.shortDescription: Instance method or constructor call nodes in expression trees have a child labeled "this"

misconceptions.name: ThisExistsInStaticMethod
misconceptions.shortDescription: this is a local variable, also in static methods

misconceptions.name: ThisInConstructorIsNull
misconceptions.shortDescription: In a constructor, this is null

misconceptions.name: ThisNoExpression
misconceptions.shortDescription: The name this is not an expression

misconceptions.name: ToStringPrints
misconceptions.shortDescription: Invoking toString() prints something

misconceptions.name: TryCatchMandatory
misconceptions.shortDescription: If code could throw an exception, you must surround that code with a try/catch block

misconceptions.name: TryFinishes
misconceptions.shortDescription: Exceptions get thrown at the end of the try block

misconceptions.name: UndeclaredVariables
misconceptions.shortDescription: Variables don't need to be declared

misconceptions.name: UnqualifiedNamesMustDiffer
misconceptions.shortDescription: The unqualified names of different classes must be different

misconceptions.name: UntypedVariables
misconceptions.shortDescription: Variable declarations don't need a type

misconceptions.name: UseOfSelfTypeImpliesRecursiveType
misconceptions.shortDescription: If a class has a method that has a local variable, parameter, or return value with the class as its type, the class is a recursive type

misconceptions.name: VariablesHoldExpressions
misconceptions.shortDescription: = stores an expression in a variable

misconceptions.name: VariablesHoldObjects
misconceptions.shortDescription: A variable of a reference type contains a whole object

misconceptions.name: VoidMethodNotRecursive
misconceptions.shortDescription: A method with void return type can't be recursive

misconceptions.name: VoidMethodReturnsValue
misconceptions.shortDescription: A method with void return type can return a value

misconceptions.name: ZeroDigitsCompress
misconceptions.shortDescription: In integer numbers, decimal digits with value `0` take less storage than decimal digits with other values

misconceptions.name: ZeroInEdges
misconceptions.shortDescription: A control-flow graph node (other than the entry node) can have zero incoming edges

misconceptions.name: ZeroOutEdges
misconceptions.shortDescription: A control-flow graph node (other than the exit node) can have zero outgoing edges

misconceptions.name: AccessingInexistentPropertyError
misconceptions.shortDescription: Accessing a non existent property on an object produces an error

misconceptions.name: ArrowFunctionNoImpliedReturn
misconceptions.shortDescription: Even when an arrow function consists just of an expression, the return keyword must be explicitly written

misconceptions.name: ArrowFunctionRequiresFunctionKeyword
misconceptions.shortDescription: Arrow functions also require the keyword 'function'

misconceptions.name: AssignmentCopiesObject
misconceptions.shortDescription: Assignment copies the object

misconceptions.name: CallbackParametersInCaller
misconceptions.shortDescription: Parameters of a callback function may be written as parameters of the caller function

misconceptions.name: CharType
misconceptions.shortDescription: A single character is of type char

misconceptions.name: ClassDefinesType
misconceptions.shortDescription: The type of an object is equivalent to the type defined by its class definition

misconceptions.name: ConditionalOperatorNotExpression
misconceptions.shortDescription: The conditional operator is not an expression

misconceptions.name: ConstDeclarationCanBeLeftUninitialized
misconceptions.shortDescription: Declarations of constants do not need to be immediately initialized

misconceptions.name: ConstReferenceImpliesImmutability
misconceptions.shortDescription: An object referred to by a const variable is an immutable object

misconceptions.name: EqualityOperatorComparesObjectsValues
misconceptions.shortDescription: The equality operator compares two objects' values

misconceptions.name: EqualityOperatorComparesOnlyTypes
misconceptions.shortDescription: The equality operator '==' compares only the types of the operands

misconceptions.name: FunctionAsValueWithParentheses
misconceptions.shortDescription: To use a function as a value, one needs to have parentheses after its name

misconceptions.name: FunctionOverloading
misconceptions.shortDescription: It is possible to create multiple functions with the same name but with different signatures

misconceptions.name: FunctionsCannotBeImmediatelyInvoked
misconceptions.shortDescription: Functions cannot be called in the expression in which they are defined

misconceptions.name: FunctionsMustBeNamed
misconceptions.shortDescription: Every function definition requires an associated name

misconceptions.name: IdentifierAsStringInBracketNotation
misconceptions.shortDescription: An identifier used to access a property with the bracket notation is treated as a string

misconceptions.name: MandatoryAssignment
misconceptions.shortDescription: An expression must be assigned to have a valid statement

misconceptions.name: MapInPlace
misconceptions.shortDescription: Map modifies the elements of the array on which it operates in place

misconceptions.name: NoAtomicExpression
misconceptions.shortDescription: Expressions must consist of more than one piece

misconceptions.name: NoBracketNotationForObjects
misconceptions.shortDescription: Square brackets cannot be used to access properties of an object

misconceptions.name: NoFunctionCallsChaining
misconceptions.shortDescription: It is not allowed to chain function calls

misconceptions.name: NoGlobalObject
misconceptions.shortDescription: There isn't a global object

misconceptions.name: NoReturnValue
misconceptions.shortDescription: Functions without return statements return no value at all

misconceptions.name: NullAndUndefinedAreTheSame
misconceptions.shortDescription: The values null and undefined are the same

misconceptions.name: NullIsObject
misconceptions.shortDescription: null is an object

misconceptions.name: NumberOfParametersMatchArguments
misconceptions.shortDescription: Functions must be called with the same number of arguments as defined in their signature

misconceptions.name: ObjectAsParameterIsCopied
misconceptions.shortDescription: Objects are passed by value

misconceptions.name: PrototypesAreClasses
misconceptions.shortDescription: JavaScript is based on a class-based object model

misconceptions.name: SetTimeout0IsSynchronous
misconceptions.shortDescription: Scheduling the execution of a function after 0 milliseconds with setTimeout is equivalent to a synchronous call

misconceptions.name: SetTimeoutReturnsCallbackResult
misconceptions.shortDescription: SetTimeout returns the value returned by the callback function

misconceptions.name: StringRepetitionOperator
misconceptions.shortDescription: One can repeat a String by multiplying it with a number

misconceptions.name: ThisAssignable
misconceptions.shortDescription: One can assign to this

misconceptions.name: TypeofArrayIsArray
misconceptions.shortDescription: The typeof operator applied on an array returns 'array'

misconceptions.name: TypeofNullIsNull
misconceptions.shortDescription: The value null is of type 'null'

misconceptions.name: NoAtomicExpression
misconceptions.shortDescription: Expressions must consist of more than one piece

misconceptions.name: NoSequenceRepetition
misconceptions.shortDescription: There is no operator that repeats sequences

misconceptions.name: PlusConcatenatesNumbers
misconceptions.shortDescription: The plus operator can concatenate strings and numbers
